summary:
Cars are driving toward a destination at position target on a single-lane road, so no car can pass another. There are n cars; position[i] gives the starting location of car i and speed[i] gives its constant speed. A faster car that catches up to a slower one ahead slows down and travels at the slower car's speed from then on, forming a group.

A car fleet is a set of one or more cars bunched together that arrive at the destination at the same time (a single car alone also counts as a fleet). If a car reaches the target at the exact same moment as a fleet ahead, it joins that fleet. Return how many distinct fleets reach the destination.

examples.explanation: The cars starting at 10 and 8 meet and travel together as one fleet; the car at 0 stays alone; the cars at 5 and 3 form another fleet, giving 3 fleets in total.
A single car forms one fleet on its own.

constraints: 0 <= position[i] < target and all positions are distinct
